items.title: 'No genuine opportunity' to prevent crash that killed two women, coroner finds

children.content: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

gallery.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

thumbnail.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

image.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers

children.content: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

image.altText: two large cuttlefish near rocks underwater

items.title: Randall says concussion left her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

image.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast

children.content: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

image.altText: A bunch of flowers placed next to a road.

items.title: Tourism operator says 'silence is deafening' about how to spot bird flu cases

children.content: A tourism operator on South Australia's coast is frustrated by what he says is poor communication from authorities about bird flu. He says he saw sick birds weeks ago but did not know to report the sighting.

gallery.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

thumbnail.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

items.title: 'We don't fully understand': The footy risk few parents grasp

children.content: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

image.altText: Players compete for the ball at a junior Australian Rules football match.

avatar.alt: Portrait of ABC journalist Jessica Halloran.

items.title: Thousands of outback roads damaged by floods could take year to fix

children.content: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

image.altText: A man holding a tape measure down the edge of a washed out dirt road

items.title: Network 10 strikes last-minute deal to extend broadcasting in SA and NSW

children.content: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a tower with trees visible

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

children.content: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

image.altText: A man holds a toddler while a woman speaks into media microphones outside a house

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

image.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting

items.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

image.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

images.altText: Morocco players celebrate after winning penalty shootout against the Netherlands.
Ismael Saibari and Jan-Paul van Hecke stand nose-to-nose
Germany players react as a Paraguay player celebrates in the foreground.

articles.title: Lifeless Germany fall at World Cup as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day

children.content: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

image.altText: Orlando Gill screams towards the crowd while players celebrate behind him

articles.title: Quick hits: Shootout drama, VAR controversy make for German nightmare

children.content: The knockout stages of the World Cup are in full swing, with penalty shootout drama and VAR controversy already featuring. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Kai Havertz, German fan and Gustavo Gomez

articles.title: How a punt on a trial in Italy created the Socceroos' next star

children.content: Cristian Volpato was rejected by two Australian soccer academies. His family dropped everything to go for a trial in Italy. That was the making of Australia's next star.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

children.content: Morocco stays alive in the World Cup after defeating the Netherlands in a pulsating sudden-death penalty shootout.

items.title: Morocco advances after epic penalty shootout win over Netherlands

updates.title: FT: Penalties will decide this one

items.title: Why Telstra's coverage map shrank today

image.altText: Deb Bewick holds a mobile phone up in search of reception

children.content: Australia's major telcos are being forced to comply with new standards when promoting mobile coverage networks, and Telstra is not happy.

items.title: Liberal frontbenchers rebuff call from colleague to rebrand party

children.content: Liberal frontbenchers have rebuffed calls from shadow minister Melissa McIntosh to rebrand the Liberal Party. Follow live.

image.altText: Tim Wilson looks concerned and peers to the side.

items.title: Lives would have been saved if armed Jewish guards were at Bondi, Lowy says

children.content: Prominent Jewish businessman and philanthropist Steven Lowy says the "tragedy would have been far smaller" if armed security were at the event targeted in the Bondi Beach shooting.

image.altText: Man in suit behind microphone, grey background

items.title: Police say 'miracle' no-one killed as six trucks destroyed in fiery crash

children.content: Police say it is a "miracle" no-one was seriously harmed after six semi-trailers were destroyed by fire after trucks crashed into a rest area south-west of Goulburn.

image.altText: A fireball caused by a burning truck in the dark.

gallery.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

thumbnail.altText: rfs truck with blaze behind it on highway road

items.title: The government is making major changes to universities. Here's what to expect

children.content: The government will spend an extra $3.6 billion over a decade on new university places, but some are warning the money could be wasted.

image.altText: University generic showing a close up of a sign saying Uni on a building.

items.title: ASX trading sideways, Wall Street jumps as AI-related stocks rebound

children.content: The Australian share market has started its day relatively flat despite a rebound in oil prices ahead of the next round of US-Iran peace talks. On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a record high. Follow live.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: Private equity boss banned from driving over 'absolute shocker' crash

children.content: Private equity baron Joel Thickins has had his license suspended after pleading guilty to negligent driving while under the influence of alcohol and refusing a breath test.

image.altText: Sydney car crash aftermath

items.title: Suspected saltwater crocodile takes dog at unexpected outback location

children.content: Richmond Shire Mayor John Wharton believes a dog has been taken by a saltwater crocodile in the Flinders River, hundreds of kilometres inland from its usual habitat.

image.altText: The Flinders River at the Bottom Crossing near Richmond.

items.title: Just because I am a brown woman doesn't mean I know all the rules

children.content: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

gallery.altText: Sashi Perera stands next to her husband Charlie, wearing a burnt orange sari. Charlie wears a suit.

thumbnail.altText: Sashi Perera stands next to her husband Charlie, wearing a burnt orange sari. Charlie wears a suit.

avatar.alt: Portrait of writer and comedian Sashi Perera wearing a long shirt dress that is pale purple, with a green skirt.

items.title: More Australians are doing their own tax returns and making these mistakes

children.content: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.

image.altText: woman looking at laptop on the couch with paperwork

items.title: BalletTok, blackface and nudes: Inside the world of professional ballet

children.content: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

image.altText: Woman with long dark hair sitting wearing white and looking at camera

items.title: Nervous wait for snow after 'terrible' start to NSW ski season

children.content: Snow may finally be on the way as NSW ski resorts ride out a slow start to the season.

gallery.altText: a screenshot of patchy snow on a hill

thumbnail.altText: a screenshot of patchy snow on a hill

items.title: ACCC sues Amazon over introduction of ads on Prime

children.content: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon over alleged unfair contract terms for its Prime streaming service.

image.altText: An amazon logo is seen on a building

items.title: Major coal mine to close in 2028 with hundreds of jobs lost

children.content: Hundreds of jobs will be lost over the next 18 months as a major coal operation in the New South Wales Upper Hunter begins a staged closure.

image.altText: A man holds out his hand with a small lump of coal in it. He is wearing hi-vis workwear.

items.title: Ill-fated midnight mountain walk in freezing conditions led to 12-day search

children.content: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

gallery.altText: A woman with blond hair smiling at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with blond hair smiling at the camera.

items.title: Concern as private health giant wins access to public emergency patients

children.content: A trial to ease pressure on a public emergency department has raised concerns a multi-million-dollar company is getting a special deal.

image.altText: Emergency department signage at the Lyell McEwin Hospital.

items.title: 'Systemic racism' tip of the iceberg in buried reef authority review

children.content: The damning results of a review into the agency tasked with managing the Great Barrier Reef, an area bigger than New Zealand and home to 70 traditional owner groups, has never before seen the light of day.

image.altText: Colourful reef swimming around coral reef.

items.title: NDIS shell company boom sparks crackdown

children.content: The federal government says it is moving to crack down on the growing trade in "off-the-shelf" NDIS providers.

image.altText: A middle aged white woman with short fair hair and red suit sitting in an office chair

items.title: Regional council boss resigns after very difficult six-year tenure

children.content: During that time, Bradley Thomas has faced a "private prosecution" by a ratepayer and had a cup of coffee thrown at him by a resident.

image.altText: Grey haired white man in checkered shirt and dark blazer, sitting in front of door that says Mayor's room.

items.title: New fellowship for mid-career journalists honours Judith Whelan

children.content: The ABC, The Sydney Morning Herald and Sydney University have created a new fellowship to honour the late Judith Whelan, a trailblazing leader in media.

image.altText: A blonde woman smiling with black round glasses.

items.title: Court rejects Trump's final bid to overturn E Jean Carroll verdict

children.content: The US Supreme Court declined to hear US President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5 million USD verdict in a case in which a jury found him liable for sexually abusing and defaming E Jean Carroll.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request government help as quake toll climbs

children.content: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.
